This classification places Meiocarpidium lepidotum among other plants that share similar characteristics and morphological features. The plant's classification is further refined to the genus Meiocarpidium, which is a monotypic genus containing only this single species. Within the kingdom Plantae, Meiocarpidium lepidotum is also assigned to the subkingdom Viridiplantae, indicating its status as a vascular plant. The specific classification of Meiocarpidium lepidotum reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plant species within the Apiaceae family.

intro: Meiocarpidium lepidotum, also known as Guduchi or Amrita, is a woody climber or shrub native to the tropical forests of Asia, belonging to the family Apocynaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, which can help to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of various diseases. The bioactive constituents of Meiocarpidium lepidotum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Meiocarpidium lepidotum is commonly prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often used to treat conditions such as malaria, tuberculosis, and viral infections.
